answer: The authentic narrations strictly mandate caution regarding *takfir* by warning of its severe spiritual consequences. The Prophet taught that whenever a person calls his brother an unbeliever, the accusation of unbelief will inevitably return to at least one of them [1]. If the accused person is truly an unbeliever, the statement holds true; however, if the accusation is false, the charge of unbelief rebounds directly upon the individual who made the false claim [1]. This establishes a definitive warning against hastily or falsely excommunicating a fellow Muslim.
